content:
The Express RECO module is a
web-based reconciliation solution designed to facilitate accurate and efficient
comparison of Books data with 26AS/AIS (Annual Information Statement).

The module is accessible via:

app.kdksoftware.com
expresstds.com

It provides a structured workflow
for client creation, party mapping, transaction mapping, and reconciliation,
ensuring data accuracy, transparency, and compliance.

Key
Features
1.
26AS/AIS Reconciliation – Books vs. 26AS/AIS
Express RECO enables seamless
reconciliation between Books and 26AS/AIS data, allowing users to identify
discrepancies, validate transactions, and ensure accurate reporting.

2.
Client Creation
The system offers a simplified and
secure process for creating and managing clients:
User-friendly interface for quick client onboarding
Minimal steps for efficient client setup
In-built validations to ensure accurate data entry
Secure framework to protect sensitive client
     information
3.
Party Mapping

Party Mapping ensures accurate
alignment of entities between Books and 26AS/AIS:
Enables precise mapping of parties across both data sources
Reduces reconciliation errors through structured
     mapping
Provides detailed reports for audit and compliance
     purposes 

Allows smooth navigation and review of mapped records
4.
Transaction Mapping
Transaction Mapping provides
detailed control and flexibility in reconciliation:
Categorization of transactions based on:
Section
Transaction Date
Tax Value
Status
Detailed transaction-level insights for better analysis
Identification of missing or unmatched transactions
Configurable tolerance limits as per business
     requirements
Improved accuracy through customizable mapping criteria
5.
Reconciliation & Analysis
The module provides comprehensive
insights to support decision-making:
Consolidated view of reconciled and unreconciled
     transactions
Advanced filtering based on status and matching
     parameters
Risk identification between Books and 26AS/AIS data
Enables follow-up actions with Deductors for TDS
     compliance
Intuitive interface for easy data review and navigation
6.
Navigation & Usability
Express RECO is designed for ease of
use and quick implementation:
Direct access to the Reconciliation Summary
     Dashboard
Smooth and consistent navigation across all modules
Optimized interface for faster processing and minimal
     learning curve

For more details and related Knowledgebase articles, please refer to: https://support.kdksoftware.com/portal/en/kb/kdk-softwares/express-reco

The Express RECO module
provides a comprehensive and user-friendly solution for 26AS/AIS
reconciliation. By combining structured workflows, intelligent mapping, and
actionable insights, it enables users to improve accuracy, ensure compliance,
and streamline reconciliation processes effectively.

summary: To ensure the highest level of security for your account, we strongly recommend enabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) across all our applications, including ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS, and ExpressReco. With 2FA enabled, a single One-Time ...
content:
To ensure the highest level of security for your account, we strongly recommend enabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) across all our applications, including ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS, and ExpressReco.

With 2FA enabled, a single One-Time Password (OTP) generated through the Google Authenticator app will be used for authentication across all these applications, ensuring seamless yet secure access.
​

Why Enable 2FA?

2FA provides an additional layer of protection by requiring a One-Time Password (OTP) generated via the Google Authenticator app. This extra security measure helps prevent unauthorized access, even if your password is compromised.

​

Steps to Enable 2FA:

Access Security Settings: Navigate to your Profile and select the Security option.

Initiate Setup: Click on "Set Up Now" to begin the activation process.

Install Google Authenticator : Download the Google Authenticator app on your mobile device.

Scan the QR Code: Use the authenticator app to scan the QR code displayed on your screen.

Enter the OTP: Retrieve the generated OTP from the app and enter it to complete the setup.

​

How 2FA Works During Login:

Once enabled, every login attempt will require an OTP from Google Authenticator. Simply open the app, retrieve the latest code, and enter it to access your account. 

      

Without the correct OTP, login will be denied.

      
​

​

The Importance of 2FA:

By activating 2FA, you are significantly enhancing your account’s security, making it nearly impossible for unauthorized users to gain access — even if they have your password. This is a crucial step in protecting your personal data and preventing security breaches.

🔒 Secure your account today by enabling 2FA.

tags: online correction - add challan to statement

content:
This feature allows the deductor to add a challan to an already filed statement. The Add Challan functionality is not available through offline correction. If the statement is not processed by TRACES, please file C1 (Personal Information) through the Conso file. Once processed, you can proceed with online correction on TRACES.

Traces e-Tutorials Links - Click Here !

Steps for Online Correction:

Step 1: Login to the TRACES Website

Log in to the ExpressTDS portal using your username and password. The Deductor Master page will open, displaying a list of all deductors.

From the Deductor Master, click on the desired deductor to access their details page.

In the selected deductor's page, review the relevant information.

Locate the TRACES icon in the deductor's page.

      
Click on the TRACES icon to be redirected to the TRACES portal login page.

      

Step 2: Navigate to Request for Correction under the Defaults menu.

      

      
Step 3: Enter the relevant details: Financial Year, Quarter, Form Type, and select Correction Category as "Online". Click on the Submit Request button.

      

      
The request will be available under Track Correction Request.

Once the request status changes to Available, click on Available/In Progress to proceed with the correction.

      

Step 4: Validate KYC
Note: The Digital Signature-supported KYC validation screen will appear only if the Digital Signature is registered.

      

      

Normal KYC

      

      

      

      

Step 5: After successful KYC validation, select the Type of Correction Category from the dropdown as Add Challan to Statement.

      

      

Click on Add Challan (Minor Head Code-200/400)

      

      

      

      

      

      

User can remove added challan through ‘Remove Challan’ option.

      

Note: Only newly added challans can be removed from the statement. Challans that were already present in the statement cannot be removed.

      

      

Add Challan (Minor Head Code-100)

      

      

      

      

      

Step 6: Make the required corrections in the selected file and click on Submit for Processing to submit the correction. (Only available to the Admin User)

      

      

      

      

      

Step 7: A 15-digit token number will be generated and sent to the registered email ID.

      

      

      

      

By following this process, you can successfully add a challan to the statement via online correction.
